title: trifolium mutabile subsp. gussoneanum
intro: Trifolium mutabile subsp. gussoneanum, also known as Gussone's clover, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae. Traditionally, Gussone's clover has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Gussone's clover include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its antioxidant properties, which may help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Gussone's clover is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to support overall health and well-being.
